# MLX-Gen Full Documentation Context

## Project Summary

MLX-Gen is an MLX-native generative image and video runtime forked from mflux and published as the `mlx-gen` Python package. It is currently built on the mflux codebase while adding independent release cadence, Qwen Image/Edit support, ERNIE Image Turbo support, initial Wan2.2 text-to-video and first-frame image-to-video support, FLUX.2 fixes, quantized model packaging, local model loading behavior, generated Hugging Face model cards, and AbstractVision integration needs.

Some internals currently remain under `mflux.*`; new application code should use the `mlxgen` command and import path.

## Document Index

- `README.md`: installation, quick start, model families, project relationship, and acknowledgements.
- `docs/getting-started.md`: install, inspect `mlxgen --help`, prepare model files, generate images, edit images, and run Wan video examples with duration/FPS/resolution guidance.
- `docs/architecture.md`: package shape, command boundaries, model-file lifecycle, runtime failure contract, and Python integration boundary.
- `docs/api.md`: public `mlxgen` command surface, generation router, model-management commands, and Python integration boundary.
- `docs/model-management.md`: cache-only runtime policy, `download` versus `prepare`, Depth Pro, and `DownloadRequiredError`.
- `docs/quantization.md`: MLX-Gen prepared folder compatibility by model family, validation panels, and current Qwen/ERNIE mixed q4/q8 policies.
- `docs/huggingface-publishing.md`: generated model cards, source license/access wording, default `AbstractFramework/<repo-name>` usage, upload flow, and optional collection membership.
- `docs/python-integration.md`: in-process use, AbstractVision integration, progress limitations, and threading guidance.
- `docs/faq.md`: common questions about `prepare`, downloads, package naming, and compatibility.
- `docs/troubleshooting.md`: missing files, local path classification, output replacement, `generate --path`, ERNIE Prompt Enhancer files, ERNIE small-resolution and unsupported edit-input behavior, Wan video quality-setting limits, LoRA, and `hf_transfer`.
- `docs/release.md`: GitHub Release and PyPI trusted publishing workflow.
- `ACKNOWLEDGEMENTS.md`: upstream mflux credit and post-fork maintainership.
- `CONTRIBUTING.md`: development setup, checks, documentation expectations, and pull-request scope.
- `SECURITY.md`: vulnerability reporting and model/token safety guidance.
- `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md`: participation expectations.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: user-visible release history.

---

## Relationship To mflux

MLX-Gen started as a fork of `filipstrand/mflux`. Most credit for the current codebase goes to Filip Strand and the original mflux contributors. Changes after the fork are maintained in this project. The project keeps mflux vocabulary where useful for compatibility and possible upstream merge-back, while it may diverge as its own package.

See `ACKNOWLEDGEMENTS.md` for upstream mflux credit, post-fork maintainership, and model/community acknowledgements.

---

## Architecture

MLX-Gen keeps the MLX-native model runtime inherited from mflux while exposing a cleaner `mlxgen` command surface for new users and applications. The first supported video paths are Wan2.2 TI2V text-to-video and experimental first-frame image-to-video.

The package shape is:

- PyPI distribution: `mlx-gen`
- Public CLI root: `mlxgen`
- New application import identity: `mlxgen`
- Current runtime internals: primarily `mflux.*`

The public command boundary separates setup from inference:

- `mlxgen download` explicitly populates the local cache.
- `mlxgen prepare` explicitly creates a local model folder.
- `mlxgen generate` runs inference and does not start downloads by default.

Prepared folders use the MLX/mflux saved-weight layout and may contain MLX quantization tensors. They are intended for MLX-Gen and compatible mflux code, not direct Diffusers or Transformers loading.

---

## Runtime Download Policy

MLX-Gen generation is cache-only by default. It will not download model weights, tokenizers, LoRAs, or Depth Pro weights during generation or ordinary Python model construction.

This is required for embedded workflow engines such as AbstractVision: a complex job should not unexpectedly start a large network transfer. Missing files raise `DownloadRequiredError`, which is also a `FileNotFoundError` for compatibility.

The exception exposes:

- `repo_id`
- `artifact`
- `download_command`
- `prepare_command` when a local prepared folder is applicable

---

## Public CLI

The public command surface is:

- `mlxgen generate`: generate images, edit images, or generate supported videos from cached or prepared files.
- `mlxgen download`: explicitly download a model snapshot, LoRA repository, or supported direct-download artifact into the local cache.
- `mlxgen prepare`: create a reusable local MLX-Gen model folder, optionally quantized, and write a Hugging Face `README.md` model card.

Use `mlxgen --help` for the top-level command list and `mlxgen <command> --help` for command-specific options.

`mlxgen prepare` is the public MLX-Gen workflow for creating local quantized model folders. There is no separate MLX-Gen `save` workflow in the user-facing documentation.

---

## Explicit Model Management

Download a Hugging Face snapshot into the local cache:

```sh
mlxgen download --model Qwen/Qwen-Image
```

Download a known alias:

```sh
mlxgen download --model z-image-turbo
```

Prepare a reusable local MLX-Gen folder:

```sh
mlxgen prepare \
  --model Qwen/Qwen-Image \
  --path ./models/qwen-image-8bit \
  --quantize 8
```

Download Depth Pro weights from Apple:

```sh
mlxgen download --model depth-pro
```

`mlxgen download` only fills the local cache. `mlxgen prepare` creates a reusable local folder at `--path`, applies the requested quantization when `--quantize` is provided, and writes a generated model card. The model card records the `mlx-gen` version that generated it, defaults usage examples to `AbstractFramework/<repo-name>`, and includes `python -m pip install -U mlx-gen` for copy/pasteable public installation.

`HF_HUB_ENABLE_HF_TRANSFER=1` is optional acceleration for explicit Hugging Face download/prepare commands. It is not required to authorize network access.

---

## Generation

Use `mlxgen generate` as the smart router:

```sh
mlxgen generate \
  --model z-image-turbo \
  --prompt "A puffin standing on a cliff" \
  --width 1280 \
  --height 500 \
  --seed 42 \
  --steps 9 \
  --quantize 8
```

For image editing:

```sh
mlxgen generate \
  --model AbstractFramework/qwen-image-edit-2511-4bit \
  --image input.png \
  --prompt "Turn the room into a pencil sketch" \
  --steps 20 \
  --seed 42 \
  --output edited.png
```

If a local model path or custom repository name cannot be classified, pass `--family qwen`, `--family flux2`, `--family fibo`, `--family z-image`, `--family ernie-image`, or `--family wan`.

ERNIE Image Turbo is also routed by `mlxgen generate`:

```sh
mlxgen generate \
  --model baidu/ERNIE-Image-Turbo \
  --prompt "A clean product photo of a ceramic mug" \
  --width 512 \
  --height 512 \
  --steps 8 \
  --guidance 1 \
  --output image.png
```

For local ERNIE folders whose names cannot be classified, pass `--family ernie-image`. The ERNIE port supports text-to-image plus experimental single-image image-to-image with BF16 source weights and prepared q8/q4 folders. ERNIE Prompt Enhancer is available with `--use-prompt-enhancer` when a full source snapshot with `pe/` and `pe_tokenizer/` files is present. ERNIE multi-image edit is not supported. Width or height below 384px is not recommended because very small ERNIE outputs can crop or truncate subjects. For ERNIE image-to-image, preserve the source aspect ratio, use `--image-strength 0.25` to `0.35` for visible stylization, `0.45` to `0.6` for stronger source preservation, and Qwen Image Edit when exact source layout preservation matters.

`mlxgen generate` does not accept `--path` to create model folders. Use `mlxgen prepare --model ... --path ... --quantize ...` for model preparation and use `--output` to choose the generated image or video path.

Wan2.2 TI2V routes through the same command surface for text-to-video. Use the upstream TI2V-5B scale for visual quality validation:

```sh
mlxgen generate \
  --model Wan-AI/Wan2.2-TI2V-5B-Diffusers \
  --task text-to-video \
  --prompt "A short cinematic video of a glowing orange glass sphere floating above teal water" \
  --width 1280 \
  --height 704 \
  --frames 121 \
  --steps 50 \
  --guidance 5 \
  --fps 24 \
  --output video.mp4
```

Wan image-to-video uses the Diffusers first-frame latent-conditioning path rather than ordinary image-to-image latent initialization. It VAE-encodes one input frame, masks first-frame timesteps, and reinserts the condition before decode. Multi-image/video interpolation is not enabled.

Example image-to-video command:

```sh
mlxgen generate \
  --model Wan-AI/Wan2.2-TI2V-5B-Diffusers \
  --task image-to-video \
  --image input.png \
  --prompt "A slow cinematic camera move from the input frame" \
  --width 1280 \
  --height 704 \
  --frames 121 \
  --steps 50 \
  --guidance 5 \
  --fps 24 \
  --output video.mp4
```

Wan does not have a separate duration flag. Duration is `frames / fps`; at 24 fps, 121 frames is about 5.04 seconds, 73 frames is about 3.04 seconds, and 49 frames is about 2.04 seconds. Frame counts are normalized to `4n + 1`. Width and height must be at least 32 pixels and are normalized to multiples of 32, so 1280x720 becomes 1280x704. Use 1280x704 for landscape or 704x1280 for portrait quality validation. Spatial-scale T2V and I2V sanity panels at 1280x704, 17 frames, and 20 steps live in `docs/assets/generation/`; they are coherent non-green outputs, not full quality benchmarks.

Generation commands replace the requested `--output` path by default. Use `--replace false` or `--no-replace` to preserve an existing file and save to the next suffixed path.

---

## Quantization

MLX-Gen prepares quantized model folders with the mflux/MLX layout used for local inference. These folders are designed for MLX-Gen and compatible mflux code, not Diffusers or Transformers `from_pretrained()` checkpoints.

The primary q8/q4 prepared-folder compatibility surface covers Qwen Image, Qwen Image Edit, ERNIE Image Turbo, FLUX.2 Klein, Z-Image/Z-Image Turbo, and FIBO when source access is available. Qwen and ERNIE q4 folders use mixed q4/q8 policies because fully q4 checkpoints showed unacceptable generation-quality loss. FLUX.2 and Z-Image use standard MLX quantization policies. q8 remains the closest quantized option to BF16 when memory allows.

Qwen Image and Qwen Image Edit use a mixed q4/q8 policy when prepared with `--quantize 4`:

- q4 for most Qwen transformer attention, feed-forward, and projection linears.
- q8 for Qwen `*.img_mod_linear` transformer modulation layers.
- q4 for group64-compatible Qwen text-encoder language linears.
- q8 for group64-compatible Qwen text-encoder visual linears.
- BF16 for the VAE, norms, embeddings, and linears that are not MLX group64-compatible.

This Qwen policy applies to q4 prepared folders only, including Qwen Image and Qwen Image Edit variants such as 2509 and 2511 edit checkpoints.

The q8 path was not changed by the mixed-q4 work. Qwen q8 uses the standard MLX-Gen/mflux quantization flow: quantizable modules are saved at 8-bit where supported, while VAE weights and non-quantizable layers remain BF16. Other model families use their existing model-specific quantization predicates.

ERNIE Image Turbo supports BF16, q8, and q4 text-to-image generation plus experimental single-image image-to-image. ERNIE image-to-image initializes text-to-image denoising from encoded image latents, so it is useful for fast stylization and guided variation but not as exact as Qwen Image Edit for layout-preserving edits. ERNIE q4 uses a model-specific mixed q4/q8 policy: q4 for transformer Q/K attention and transformer feed-forward modules; q8 for Mistral3 text/Prompt Enhancer linears plus ERNIE transformer V/O attention, conditioning/output linears, and VAE attention; BF16 for norms, convolutions, and other non-quantizable parameters. Default ERNIE downloads fetch the BF16 generation components MLX-Gen currently uses and do not fetch the Prompt Enhancer model unless `mlxgen download --all-files` is requested. Prepared ERNIE q8/q4 folders contain the generation stack for text-to-image and experimental single-image image-to-image, and do not bundle Prompt Enhancer files.

---

## Hugging Face Publishing

Prepared model folders include a generated Hugging Face `README.md` card. The card records the source model, compatibility notes, quantization policy, exact `mlx-gen` version used to generate it, and source license/access policy where MLX-Gen can infer it. Generated cards include `python -m pip install -U mlx-gen` in the usage block for copy/pasteable public installation; repository development and release workflows still use uv. Upload the folder with a command such as:

```sh
huggingface-cli upload AbstractFramework/qwen-image-edit-2511-4bit ./models/qwen-image-edit-2511-4bit .
```

Hugging Face collection membership is managed separately from model-card metadata. After upload, use the Hugging Face UI or `huggingface_hub.HfApi.add_collection_item` with the collection slug from the collection URL.

Generated cards mark Qwen, Z-Image, ERNIE Image Turbo, and FLUX.2 Klein 4B derivatives as Apache 2.0 when MLX-Gen can identify the source model family. FLUX.2 Klein 9B and FLUX.2 Klein base-9B derivatives are marked with the FLUX non-commercial license metadata and gated-access prompts. Configure Hugging Face repository gating separately when publishing those derivatives; model-card metadata alone does not enforce access control.

---

## Python Integration

Python callers should download or prepare models before constructing runtime model objects.

```python
from mflux.models.common.download_policy import DownloadRequiredError
from mlxgen.models.z_image import ZImageTurbo

try:
    model = ZImageTurbo(quantize=8)
except DownloadRequiredError as exc:
    print(exc.download_command)
    raise
```

Applications should surface the exception message or command fields and stop the workflow.

MLX-Gen is intended to be the Apple Silicon / MLX dependency for AbstractVision while AbstractVision remains cross-platform.

Current responsibilities:

- AbstractVision owns provider-neutral request objects, artifact storage, capability checks, and AbstractCore plugin integration.
- MLX-Gen owns MLX model loading, model-family behavior, local quantized formats, and runtime compatibility fixes.
- MLX-Gen fails early when required local artifacts are missing so AbstractVision can surface an actionable remediation message.

Existing model callbacks remain available through the mflux runtime internals. A stable, backend-facing progress event API is not yet part of MLX-Gen.

---

## Troubleshooting

If MLX-Gen says it will not download files during generation, run the command shown in the error and retry.

If a local path cannot be classified, add `--family`.

If `generate --path` fails, use `mlxgen prepare --model ... --path ... --quantize ...` to create a model folder or `--output` to choose the generated image or video path.

If the requested `--output` image already exists, MLX-Gen replaces it by default. Use `--replace false` or `--no-replace` to write to the next suffixed filename instead.

If a LoRA is missing, download it or pass a local `.safetensors` path. Missing user-requested LoRAs are required and are not silently ignored.

If `HF_HUB_ENABLE_HF_TRANSFER=1` is explicitly enabled and fails because `hf_transfer` is missing, install MLX-Gen with:

```sh
uv tool install --upgrade mlx-gen --with hf_transfer
```

---

## Release

MLX-Gen publishes Python distributions to PyPI and attaches the same distributions to a GitHub Release through `.github/workflows/release.yml`.

PyPI trusted publishing must be configured for PyPI project `mlx-gen`, repository `lpalbou/mlx-gen`, workflow filename `release.yml`, and GitHub environment `pypi`. The workflow uses OpenID Connect through `pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish`; it does not require a long-lived PyPI API token.

The release workflow runs fast tests on macOS, validates package name, version, changelog entry, tag name, and duplicate PyPI versions, builds distributions with `uv build`, validates them with `twine check`, creates a GitHub Release with the wheel and source distribution when publishing, and publishes the same artifacts to PyPI through trusted publishing.

Run a non-publishing rehearsal with:

```sh
gh workflow run release.yml \
  -f version=<version> \
  -f publish=false
```

The preferred release path is a version tag:

```sh
git tag v<version>
git push origin main --tags
```

---

## Project Participation

Contributors should use `uv`, keep pull requests focused, document user-facing behavior in the core docs, and prefer `mlxgen` commands in new documentation. Wan full-model parity checks are opt-in because they require the cached source snapshot:

```sh
MFLUX_RUN_LOCAL_WAN_PARITY=1 MFLUX_PRESERVE_TEST_OUTPUT=1 uv run pytest tests/wan/test_wan_local_parity.py -q
```

Those checks validate component parity for the Wan transformer, VAE encoder/decoder, prompt embeddings, scheduler replay, and a tiny latent-only CFG denoise loop. They do not replace visual review or decoded video quality checks.

Security issues should be reported privately through the repository security advisory flow when available. Do not include secrets, private model tokens, or proprietary model artifacts in public reports.

Participants are expected to be respectful, constructive, and focused on the work.
